questions.explanation: required ensures field is present. lowercase:true auto-converts to lowercase. validate with regex checks email. minlength/maxlength restrict password length. enum restricts city to specific values only.

questions.question: Create one login form having username, password and submit button. By clicking submit button username and password should be stored inside database. Use mongoose and express module. Write all necessary files.
questions.explanation: The HTML form uses POST method. Express handles the POST route with /save. express.urlencoded parses form data into req.body. Mongoose schema defines structure, model.save() inserts into MongoDB.

questions.question: Write a program using React and Node/Express to demonstrate full stack connectivity. Take one textfield, submit button on React and insert a corresponding record in database via Node/Express script. Write required files.
questions.explanation: Full-stack app: React frontend sends POST request using axios to Express backend. Backend receives data, saves to MongoDB via Mongoose. CORS middleware allows cross-origin requests between React (port 3000) and Express (port 5000).

questions.question:
Consider a collection student having documents:
[{_id:123433,name:"DDD",age:32},{_id:123434,name:"BBB",age:20},{_id:123435,name:"AAA BBB",age:10}]
(1) Retrieve all records having "BBB" as a substring in name by indexing. Predict nReturned & docsExamined.
(2) Create an index and fire a command to retrieve documents having age>15. Stats must return nReturned=2 & docExamined=2, even though total 3 records are there.
questions.explanation: A text index allows $text search for words in strings. A partial index with partialFilterExpression only indexes a subset of documents satisfying the condition. Querying within that subset gives IXSCAN with docsExamined equal to only the indexed documents.

questions.question: Create a React form which accepts Name, Rollno and totalmarks, on submitting the form it should be saved in data table inside MongoDB database named student.
questions.explanation: React manages form state with useState. onChange updates state for each field. On submit, axios.post sends data to Express backend which saves it to MongoDB 'student' database in 'data' collection.

questions.question: Create a form containing username and submit button using .html file. After clicking on submit button, insert value of username in the database. (Note: .html and .js file required)
questions.explanation: HTML form uses POST method pointing to /save route. express.static serves static HTML file. express.urlencoded parses form body. Mongoose schema and model handle database insertion via .save().

questions.question:
Write a node.js script to define a schema having fields like name, age and gender.
Validations:
(1) name field must be required with minimum and maximum length of 4 & 12 respectively.
(2) age must accept a value from 1<=age<=35 only.
(3) gender must accept values in capital letters only and allowed values are "male" & "female" only.
questions.explanation: required with message, minlength and maxlength validate name. min and max validate age range. uppercase:true converts input to uppercase. enum with MALE/FEMALE restricts gender values after uppercase conversion.

questions.question: Write a script to have a 'NAME' text field and a submit button in Form.js file. After clicking on submit button, insert that value of text field in database named 'mydb'. (React MongoDB Connectivity)
questions.explanation: Form.js contains the React component with useState for name input. axios.post sends the name to Express backend. Server saves it to 'mydb' MongoDB database. CORS is needed because React and Express run on different ports.

questions.question:
Consider following citizens collection:
[{_id:123433,name:"DD",surname:"abc",age:61},{_id:123434,name:"LL",surname:"def",age:38},{_id:123435,name:"KK",surname:"pqr",age:29},{_id:123436,name:"ZZ",surname:"xyz",age:62}]
Insert above data in 'userdata' collection under database 'maindata' and write query to:
(1) Insert a category field with value "SeniorCitizen" having age greater than 60.
(2) Display total number of documents having age between 30 and 60 only.
questions.explanation: use maindata switches to the specified database. insertMany inserts the data. updateMany with $gt:60 adds a new 'category' field to senior citizens using $set. count() on find with range query ($gte and $lte) counts matching documents.

questions.question:
Consider collection named 'movie':
[{"title":"Inception","director":"Christopher Nolan","release_year":2010},{"title":"The Matrix","director":"The Wachowskis","release_year":1999},{"title":"The Avengers","director":"Joss Whedon","release_year":2012}]
(1) Create a subset-based index that includes only movies released after the year 2000. Write a command to get scan stage = "IXSCAN" for such movies.
questions.explanation: A partial index with partialFilterExpression only indexes documents satisfying the given condition. This saves index space and improves write performance. Querying with the same condition as the partial filter uses IXSCAN. Note: querying without that condition (e.g., all movies) would still use COLLSCAN.

questions.question:

[{_id:123456788,name:"ABC",subject:"FSD-2",age:27},{_id:123456789,name:"PQR",subject:"PYTHON-2",age:31},{_id:123456790,name:"XYZ",subject:"FSD-2",age:29}]

questions.question: Create a React form that accepts Employee Name, Employee ID, Salary, Department. On submit: Save data into MongoDB database named 'company', store in collection 'employees', display saved employees in a table below the form.

questions.question:
Consider the collection products:
[{name:"Laptop",category:"Electronics",price:70000,rating:4.5},{name:"Mobile",category:"Electronics",price:30000,rating:4.2},{name:"Chair",category:"Furniture",price:5000,rating:3.8}]
Perform:
1. Create a compound index on category and price.
2. Find products where category="Electronics" and price<60000.
3. Use explain("executionStats") and give: execution stage, nReturned, totalDocsExamined.
questions.explanation: Compound index on {category, price} supports queries that filter on both fields. The query planner uses IXSCAN because both index fields are used in the query. Only Mobile satisfies both conditions, so nReturned=1 and docsExamined=1.

questions.question:
Create Collection: product
[{name:"Laptop",category:"Electronics",price:70000,stock:10},{name:"Mobile",category:"Electronics",price:30000,stock:20},{name:"Chair",category:"Furniture",price:5000,stock:50}]
Tasks:
1. Create index only for products where stock > 15.
2. Query products with stock > 15 and explain execution stats.
3. Create compound index on category and price.
4. Query electronics products with price < 50000. Give scan stage, nReturned, docsExamined.
questions.explanation: Partial index with stock>15 only indexes Mobile and Chair. Compound index on {category,price} supports queries filtering on both. For electronics with price<50000, only Mobile matches (Laptop costs 70000), giving nReturned=1 and docsExamined=1 with IXSCAN.

questions.question:
Write a node.js script to define a schema having fields like name & age.
